subject: Project Management
title: Montreal's Olympic Stadium
description: A quick, practical guide to Montreal's Olympic Stadium.

hook.text: Montreal built an Olympic stadium for the 1976 Games. It wasn't fully finished until 1987.

buildup.text: Architect Roger Taillibert designed a retractable roof on a leaning tower. Elegant — and impossible.

discovery.text: Construction strikes, political interference, and design changes sent costs from $134M to $1.6 billion.

twist.text: Montreal taxpayers paid off the stadium debt for 30 years — through a special tobacco tax.

climax.text: The roof never worked properly. Pieces fell off. The stadium became known as 'The Big Owe.'

punchline.text: Thirty years of cigarette taxes to pay for a leaking roof.

quiz.question: How long did Montreal take to pay off its Olympic Stadium debt?
